blockingIssues.description: The action CTA is now rendered only for `submitted` and `in_review` tasks, but the same component still lets users filter to `approved` and `rejected` states. Those cards now render without the required operation button, violating the feature contract that every task card shows an action button and creating a dead-end filtered view.

The feature requires the task list component to call the list API when the component mounts and show loading/empty/error states around that request, but this component only reads `userStore.pendingReviewTasks` and exposes a manual `refreshTasks()` action. There is no `onMounted`, watcher, or initialization path in the component to invoke `userStore.loadReviewTasks()`, so opening the reviewer inbox can render stale persisted data or an immediate empty state without ever issuing the required GET request.

`startReviewerTask` only sets local store state and emits panel commands; it never invokes `reviewTaskStartReview` (or any equivalent backend mutation). Clicking `开始审核` therefore does not transition the task out of `submitted`, so the server-side workflow/history remain stale and the action is lost on refresh.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Refresh shared mission/library guidance so active reviewer-inbox behavior is documented, including expected mount-time inbox loading and start-review workflow semantics.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All three scrutiny reviews noted reviewer-inbox behavior had to be inferred from source because `.factory/library/architecture.md` and `.factory/library/user-testing.md` still describe earlier M1/M2 or designer-tracking flows.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify UI worker validation requirements for action and rendering features: targeted UI regressions should cover visible card states and required backend mutations even when broader baseline validation is noisy.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Reviews for `m3-reviewer-task-list-ui` and `m3-start-review-action` both found missed regressions that existing helper-only tests and skipped interactive checks did not catch.

rejectedObservations.observation: `ui-worker` says UI features should get a red-first test unless verification steps explicitly allow static checks only, but this feature still changed a user-facing component without any focused rendering test. The worker intentionally skipped browser/manual validation after baseline test failures and returned `partial`, which suggests the skill should better document what minimum scoped verification is still expected for UI-only visual rewrites under a broken baseline.

The repo library documents M1/M2 architecture and designer-task user testing, but nothing captures the reviewer inbox-specific rule that the same list supports status/priority filtering and reviewer-stage task cards. Reviewers had to infer behavior from `ReviewerTaskList.vue`, the pen file, and the validation contract instead of a consolidated inbox note.

The repo-level library docs still describe the mission scope as only M1-M2 and the user-testing surface as designer tracking flows, which no longer reflects active M3 reviewer-inbox work. Review workers have to infer reviewer-inbox entry points and contracts from feature files and transcripts instead of shared docs.

Mission guidance says the shared frontend health endpoint is `http://127.0.0.1:3101`, but the checked-in service healthcheck still uses `http://localhost:3101`, which can produce inconsistent validation behavior across environments.

The `ui-worker` skill still tells workers to add/redrive tests before implementation unless a narrow exception applies, but this feature reused an existing action test and skipped any browser validation. The deviation was understandable for a small interaction change, yet the skill does not explicitly push workers to verify required API-side state transitions for action features.

Project shared docs do not capture that reviewer 'start review' is expected to mutate backend workflow state via `/api/review/tasks/{id}/start-review` before opening the workbench, even though that API already exists.
